codeReview.summary: Happy-path coverage is broad, but one assertion is non-deterministic and causes intermittent failures; there is also a coverage gap for the explicit runtime budget requirement.

issues.description: `test_ssh_via_gh_link_happy_path` snapshots and asserts a transient `loading` frame (`loading` is included in both `assert_steps` and the snapshot loop at lines 209-211) immediately after a broad `wait` on 'Looking for your GitHub username'. Because this state advances asynchronously, the captured frame can already be on a later screen, making the golden at `client/tests/tuistory/setup/__snapshots__/ssh-via-gh-link/loading_80x24.txt` flaky rather than deterministic.

Expected behavior requires all three scenarios to complete in <30s combined, but the tests do not assert aggregate runtime. Each scenario sets `exit_timeout_ms` independently (lines 96, 153, 246) and harness execution allows up to 30s per run (`client/tests/tuistory/harness.py:121`), so a >30s combined regression would not be caught by assertions.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker skill warns against committing flaky snapshots but does not concretely guard against transient loading-state snapshots in tuistory happy-path tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/tui-tests-worker/SKILL.md says to fix flakes at the root, yet this feature added `loading` golden assertions in `client/tests/tuistory/setup/test_happy_path.py` (lines 157, 209-211) that race UI progression.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared validation commands do not include a flake-detection rerun pattern for tuistory scenario tests.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/services.yaml defines `test-tuistory` as a single `pytest` invocation; no repeated-run command is documented, so intermittent snapshot races can pass initial validation.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission library docs capture harness PTY/proxy constraints but not the deterministic-snapshot rule for transient loading screens.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/tuistory-harness.md documents Bun/PTY/cleanup behavior only; it does not note that loading snapshots should be avoided or explicitly synchronized to stable checkpoints.

summary: Review failed due to a blocking non-deterministic loading snapshot assertion in the SSH-via-gh happy-path scenario. Coverage of core behavior is otherwise strong, but aggregate runtime (<30s combined) is not asserted, and shared-state guidance should better codify anti-flake snapshot practices.
